data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-12T11:41:22.085Z</current_datetime>

Help me write MSTest tests for this formatting utility. I want to use the right MSTest string assertion methods like Assert.StartsWith and Assert.EndsWith to validate the output formats.

```csharp
namespace Contoso.Text;

public sealed class StringFormatter
{
    public string FormatPhoneNumber(string digits)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(digits);
        if (digits.Length != 10)
            throw new ArgumentException("Must be exactly 10 digits.", nameof(digits));
        return $"({digits[..3]}) {digits[3..6]}-{digits[6..]}";
    }

    public string BuildGreeting(string name, string title = "Mr.")
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(name);
        return $"Dear {title} {name},";
    }

    public string GenerateSlug(string title)
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(title);
        return Regex.Replace(title.Trim().ToLowerInvariant(), @"[^a-z0-9]+", "-").Trim('-');
    }

    public string FormatCurrency(decimal amount, string symbol = "$")
    {
        return $"{symbol}{amount:F2}";
    }
}
```

data.content: MSTest doesn't have `Assert.StartsWith`/`Assert.EndsWith` — the correct API is **`StringAssert.StartsWith`** and **`StringAssert.EndsWith`**. I'll write tests using those proper MSTest string assertions.
data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to write MSTest tests for a `StringFormatter` class. They specifically mentioned using `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` methods.

Let me check what MSTest assertion methods are available. In MSTest v3+, there are `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` methods. But in older versions of MSTest, these don't exist - you'd use `StringAssert.StartsWith` and `StringAssert.EndsWith`.

Let me think about this carefully. The user asked for `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith`. In MSTest:

- Traditional MSTest has `StringAssert.StartsWith()` and `StringAssert.EndsWith()`


- MSTest v3 doesn't actually include `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` as new methods - those don't exist in the standard assertion library. The string-specific assertions remain under `StringAssert`.

I need to use the correct API with `StringAssert.StartsWith` and `StringAssert.EndsWith` for the tests. I'll also need to add the `using System.Text.RegularExpressions;` directive since the source code uses `Regex`.

Now I'm planning out the test cases: for `FormatPhoneNumber`, I'll test valid 10-digit inputs, null inputs that should throw `ArgumentNullException`, wrong-length inputs that should throw `ArgumentException`, and verify the output format with the parentheses and dash. For `BuildGreeting`, I'll test the default behavior and other variations.

I'm also mapping out tests for `GenerateSlug` to handle simple titles, spaces converted to hyphens, and null/empty inputs, plus tests for `FormatCurrency` with positive amounts, custom symbols, and negative amounts. Now I'll set up the project files.

data.content:
All **27 tests pass**. Here's what was created:

**Key correction:** MSTest doesn't have `Assert.StartsWith`/`Assert.EndsWith`. The correct API is **`StringAssert.StartsWith`**, **`StringAssert.EndsWith`**, **`StringAssert.Contains`**, and **`StringAssert.Matches`**.

**Test coverage (27 tests across 4 methods):**

| Method | Tests |
|---|---|
| `FormatPhoneNumber` | exact output, `StartsWith` area code, `EndsWith` last four, `Contains` dash, null/short/long inputs |
| `BuildGreeting` | default & custom title, `StartsWith "Dear"`, `EndsWith ","`, `Contains` name, null/whitespace |
| `GenerateSlug` | lowercase, special chars, trimming, regex match, collapsed hyphens, null/empty |
| `FormatCurrency` | exact output, `StartsWith` default/custom symbol, `EndsWith ".00"`, negative, zero |

The test file is at `tests/Contoso.Text.Tests/StringFormatterTests.cs`.
